author: Austin Crawford
title: RI Hospitality Association Training class Servsafe® Food Safety Manager
summary: SERVSAFE® FOOD SAFETY MANAGER
content:
SERVSAFE® FOOD SAFETY MANAGER
Food service establishments are required by law to have a certified food safety manager on
duty at ALL times. Upon successful completion, this certification is valid in the United
States for 5 years. If you’re already certified but need to renew your license, you can join us for
the exam portion only, from 2-4:30pm.
When?
WEDNESDAY, JUNE 26TH | 8AM-4:30PM at The National Hotel 

TO REGISTER FOR SERVSAFE® FOOD SAFETY MANAGER, PLEASE CHOOSE ONE:
FULL CERTIFICATION | visit riha.cc/fullcert
RE-CERTIFICATION | visit riha.cc/recert

author: Austin Crawford
title: Easter Brunch & Egg Hunt at Avonlea
summary: This year we are holding an Easter Brunch with our Chef Brian Rusin at the Avolea, who has done it many years in the past.
content:
This year we are holding an Easter Brunch with our Chef Brian Rusin at the Avolea, who has done it many years in the past.
We are extending an invitation to all the locals who may not have someone to spend the day with or want to do something different. Brunch is buffet style from 8 to 11a.m. with mimosas! It is $15/person for brunch; children 12 and under are free. Right after brunch, we are doing an Easter egg hunt for any children that have joined us. We know church is at 10( we think). You are welcome to have brunch and return after church for the egg hunt with the kids.
RSVP is not necessary, but it is helpful to make sure we are properly prepared. You can respond at herdman.holly@gmail.com or send us a text at 434-944-0809.

title: Seal at Sandy Point- James Stover Exploration
summary: Join us for a walk to Sandy Point to see the seals.  Learn about these wonderful winter residents and other marine wildlife that can be seen this time of year.  Meet at Settler's Rock.
content:

Advance sign-up is recommended but not required. Email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Bird Banding
summary: Meet at Ocean View Pavilion. Bird banding demo program for all ages. Learn about which birds are in your backyard, why do scientists band birds, and everything you ever wanted to know about birds. Come with questions and a sense of wonder, and see birds up close – and maybe even in your hands.
content:
Meet at Ocean View Pavilion. Bird banding demo program for all ages. Learn about which birds are in your backyard, why do scientists band birds, and everything you ever wanted to know about birds. Come with questions and a sense of wonder, and see birds up close – and maybe even in your hands.

401-932-2941

title: Block Island Gardeners’ Fall Wrap-up
summary: The community is invited to the Block Island Gardeners’ Fall Wrap-up. Participants are encouraged to ask questions of fellow gardeners and discuss the “good, bad, and ugly” of this year’s garden. Light refreshments will be served. For questions, contact Maude at (203) 233-4889.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Dottie LeBeau Food Safety Class
summary: Dottie LeBeau is a retired inspector from the Rhode Island Department of Health. She is a certified Food Safety Instructor in Rhode Island as well as a certified Test Administrator and Trainer with the National Registry of Food Safety Professionals.
content:
Dottie LeBeau is a retired inspector from the Rhode Island Department of Health. She is a certified Food Safety Instructor in Rhode Island as well as a certified Test Administrator and Trainer with the National Registry of Food Safety Professionals. 

Local eating establishments come, learn and get food safety certified - includes exam.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Film Festival
summary: The Block Island Film Festival boasts notable actors, directors, screenwriters, filmmakers, student filmmakers and entertainment professionals, on the beautiful resort destination known as Block Island, located 13 miles off the coast of Rhode Island. A consortium of global industry professionals gather to mingle with contemporaries, and the public, in intimate settings, such as film screenings, spotlight programs, tribute and award ceremonies, question and answer forums, and nightly social mixers.
content:
The Block Island Film Festival boasts notable actors, directors, screenwriters, filmmakers, student filmmakers and entertainment professionals, on the beautiful resort destination known as Block Island, located 13 miles off the coast of Rhode Island. A consortium of global industry professionals gather to mingle with contemporaries, and the public, in intimate settings, such as film screenings, spotlight programs, tribute and award ceremonies, question and answer forums, and nightly social mixers.

This year’s film festival will operate all programming at the Block Island Maritime Institute and Champlin’s Marina & Resort in New Harbor, which is located at the edge of the Great Salt Pond. Tickets will be available at the door only. Please arrive at least 15 minutes prior to the beginning of each screening to purchase tickets. A full schedule of the festival’s programming is available at the Schedule tab.

title: Crazy-as-a-Coot Bird Walk
summary: A twice-monthly bird walk for birders & walkers of all abilities.
content:
A twice-monthly bird walk for birders & walkers of all abilities.

B.l. locations vary based on prevailing wind, weather conditions, and birdy hot tips. 

Call Kim for location: 401-595-7055

 

Programs are weather dependent

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Yoga workshop at Island Free Library
summary: Class ll Mind/Body. This class will focus on the nervous system and its connection to fight or flight. Learn how to release the psoas, the main muscle connected to the system of fight or flight. Please bring socks. Room B downstairs.
